question: What is the definition of the acronym ECCS?

nuscale_ch01_0189.title: BTP 6-2, Rev 3: Minimum Containment Pressure Model for PWR ECCS PerformanceAll (B.1 thru B.3)Various
nuscale_ch01_0189.summary: This section reviews NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria related to containment pressure and water chemistry for PWR ECCS performance. It notes that some criteria partially apply due to differences in NuScale's design, such as the absence of a containment spray system or sump, but relevant pH and hydrogen generation guidelines are still addressed. The minimum containment pressure model (BTP 6-2, Rev 3) is deemed not applicable, as NuScale's design prevents core uncovery during a LOCA.

nuscale_ch01_0188.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.3 Hydrogen Generation from Aluminum Corrosion
nuscale_ch01_0188.summary: The section reviews NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria regarding pH control and hydrogen generation from aluminum corrosion in emergency coolant water for pressurized water reactors. While NuScale's design lacks certain systems referenced in the criteria (such as a containment spray system or sump), the intent of the guidelines is still met due to similar water recirculation processes within the containment vessel. Some criteria, particularly those related to large LWRs with dual containment or scenarios involving core uncovery, are deemed not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, certain sensitive sections are withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used for identifying and numbering tables, figures, and pages within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. It clarifies that sensitive material is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements, while the public version excludes such content.

edges.description: Section A and Section B both discuss aspects of the NuScale design's conformance with regulatory criteria related to ECCS operation, referencing the same BTP 6-1 acceptance criteria and noting the absence of a containment spray system or sump in the NuScale design.

Section A references the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) and its conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B introduces the FSAR and explains its structure, indicating that Section A is citing or cross-referencing the document described in Section B.
Section B likely contains the definition of the acronym ECCS, which is referenced in Section A when discussing ECCS operation and requirements.

Section A discusses ECCS operation in the context of regulatory criteria, while Section B is an introductory section that likely contains the definitions of acronyms such as ECCS, connecting them through the need to define ECCS for regulatory analysis.
